task.title: Adopt Cytoscape.js for Control Room graph visualization
task.summary: Replace the custom SVG graph map prototype with a Cytoscape.js-powered interactive graph view and document the research-backed visualization decision.

goal.outcome: The Control Room Graph view uses an open-source graph visualization library instead of the hand-rolled SVG graph renderer.
acceptance: Cytoscape.js is added as a runtime dependency and served locally by the Control Room server without CDN/cloud access.
Graph view renders nodes and edges through Cytoscape.js with pan/zoom/select behavior.
Existing node kind, edge kind, scope, and search filters still control the visible graph slice.
Zoom controls use Cytoscape viewport APIs for zoom in, zoom out, fit, and reset.
Selecting a node or edge updates the source-backed inspector.
If the vendor asset cannot be served or Cytoscape is unavailable in the browser, the graph view shows a clear error instead of failing silently.
Docs record Cytoscape.js as the selected graph-rendering engine and preserve local-first/no-hosted-service constraints.
non_goals: No migration to React or other frontend framework.
No hosted visualization service or CDN dependency.
No direct hand-editing of generated graph state.

summary: TASK-063 Cytoscape.js Control Room graph renderer prototype

evidence: Smoke test fetches the local Cytoscape vendor asset, verifies HTML includes it, and verifies app JS references window.cytoscape/state.cy.
Existing Control Room model tests still assert graph node/edge data, filters, and server routes.
tester.boundary: derive tests or test-design evidence before code changes where practical

evidence: Added local vendor asset route and asset cache in Control Room server.
Replaced custom SVG graph map renderer with Cytoscape.js renderer, styling, layout selection, viewport controls, and tap selection handlers.
builder.boundary: change code until tests, roadmap acceptance, and required checks pass

test_design_evidence: Smoke test fetches the local Cytoscape vendor asset, verifies HTML includes it, and verifies app JS references window.cytoscape/state.cy.
Existing Control Room model tests still assert graph node/edge data, filters, and server routes.
code_change_evidence: Added local vendor asset route and asset cache in Control Room server.
Replaced custom SVG graph map renderer with Cytoscape.js renderer, styling, layout selection, viewport controls, and tap selection handlers.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Cytoscape.js is added as a runtime dependency and served locally by the Control Room server without CDN/cloud access.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: package.json adds dependency cytoscape ^3.33.3; Control Room serves /assets/vendor/cytoscape.min.js from nodeRequire.resolve('cytoscape/dist/cytoscape.min.js'); HTML loads that local asset before app JS; tests fetch the vendor asset and assert it contains Cytoscape.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Graph view renders nodes and edges through Cytoscape.js with pan/zoom/select behavior.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: drawGraphMap now constructs window.cytoscape with elements, style, layout, wheelSensitivity, minZoom/maxZoom, and tap handlers for nodes/edges.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Existing node kind, edge kind, scope, and search filters still control the visible graph slice.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: drawGraphMap preserves graphScope, nodeKind, edgeKind, graphSearch filtering before building Cytoscape elements.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Zoom controls use Cytoscape viewport APIs for zoom in, zoom out, fit, and reset.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: controlGraphViewport uses cy.fit, cy.reset, cy.zoom, minZoom, maxZoom, and rendered center positions.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Selecting a node or edge updates the source-backed inspector.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Cytoscape tap handlers call inspectGraphNode and inspectGraphEdge; those functions select Cytoscape elements and update the inspector with node/edge JSON and linked edges.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: If the vendor asset cannot be served or Cytoscape is unavailable in the browser, the graph view shows a clear error instead of failing silently.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: drawGraphMap checks window.cytoscape and renders a .cy-error panel naming /assets/vendor/cytoscape.min.js when unavailable.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Docs record Cytoscape.js as the selected graph-rendering engine and preserve local-first/no-hosted-service constraints.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Updated .codewiki/kb/product/uis/control-room.md and .codewiki/kb/system/control-room-ui.md with Cytoscape.js engine and local asset/no-CDN requirements.
npx tsc --noEmit passed; npm test passed including architecture, smoke, feature tests, and npm pack dry-run.

closure_brief.user_intent: Proceed after research by using an open-source graph visualization library for the Control Room graph view.
implemented_changes: Selected Cytoscape.js and documented the decision.
Added Cytoscape.js as a runtime dependency.
Served the Cytoscape browser bundle locally from package dependencies.
Replaced custom SVG Graph view renderer with Cytoscape.js nodes/edges, styles, layout, pan/zoom, selection, filters, and inspector integration.
Added tests for local vendor asset serving and Cytoscape app wiring.
acceptance_evidence: All TASK-063 acceptance criteria mapped to implementation/test/docs evidence.
non_goals_preserved: No hosted visualization service or CDN dependency.
No hand-editing of generated graph state.
remaining_risks: Real browser rendering was not manually validated in this run; use /reload then /wiki-ui for visual review.
Dense graph layout may need follow-up tuning.

risks: Cytoscape built-in cose layout may need tuning for very dense graphs; Sigma.js + Graphology or specialized layout remains a future option if scale/performance demands it.
Tests validate server/assets and code paths but do not execute a real browser rendering engine.
